title: Game of Darts Visual Walkthrough
seoTitle: Game of Darts Screenshots | BarkinMad Studios
description: A visual walkthrough of Game of Darts screens, showing setup, match flow, settings, premium access, help, and leaderboards.
summary: Game of Darts screenshots now work as a visual walkthrough, pairing each current image with what the screen shows, why it matters, and how players use it.

sections.heading: Visual walkthrough standard
paragraphs: This page uses Feature Showcase blocks rather than a legacy bottom gallery. Each screenshot explains what players are looking at, why the screen matters, and how it fits into a typical Game of Darts session.
The current website image set is used as a visual walkthrough of the public app experience.

featureShowcases.heading: Opening and setup flow
featureShowcases.summary: The opening screen introduces Game of Darts before players move into setup, player selection, and match configuration.
body: This matters because darts sessions often begin quickly: players need to know they are in the right app, then move straight into choosing a format and setting up the match.
Players use this screen as the entry point before selecting players, choosing a ruleset, and preparing the game for the board.
bullets: Sets the visual identity for the app.
Leads into the main setup and match-selection workflow.
Useful for understanding the start of a session.

image.alt: Game of Darts opening screen screenshot
image.caption: The opening screen is the first step before players configure a darts session.

featureShowcases.heading: Main menu and match setup
featureShowcases.summary: The main screen is where players begin the practical match workflow: choose who is playing, choose the format, and start scoring.
body: It matters because the setup screen has to work for quick casual games as well as longer match nights. Clear choices reduce friction before the first dart is thrown.
Players use this area to move from app launch into Match, Cricket, Snooker, Bob's 27, 1 to 20, party modes, or other supported formats.
bullets: Entry point for player and match setup.
Supports quick games and longer configured matches.
Learn how directly to mode, match type, DartBot, and scoring flows.

image.alt: Game of Darts main menu and match setup screenshot
image.caption: The main screen supports the session setup workflow before scoring begins.

featureShowcases.heading: Settings and personalisation
featureShowcases.summary: The settings screen gives players control over appearance, voice, DartBot level, Game Center posting, privacy, and access-related options.
body: This matters because darts apps are often used in different places: at home, in a venue, on an iPhone, on an iPad, with or without voice calling. Settings let players adjust the experience without changing their saved players or stats.
Players use settings to tune the app around their match environment, from caller voice preferences through to premium access and gameplay options.
bullets: Central place for appearance, audio, and gameplay preferences.
Includes controls related to DartBot and Game Center behaviour.
Keeps personalisation separate from match scoring.

image.alt: Game of Darts settings and customisation screenshot
image.caption: Settings help players tailor the app to their device, venue, and preferred match style.

featureShowcases.heading: Premium access options
featureShowcases.summary: The premium access screen explains optional unlocks such as Pro, DartBot access, and time-limited passes where available.
body: It matters because players should understand what is optional before they choose an unlock. The screen supports a clear path from free use into extra features without interrupting the basic scoring experience.
Players use this area when they want DartBot, advanced features, or a broader premium experience for regular match nights.
bullets: Shows optional access routes in one place.
Supports DartBot and premium feature discovery.
Keeps purchase information separate from scoring and statistics pages.

image.alt: Game of Darts premium access screenshot
image.caption: Premium access is presented as an optional route for players who want extra features.

featureShowcases.heading: Help and rules reference
featureShowcases.summary: The help screen supports players who need a reminder of app behaviour, rules, or setup information before or during a session.
body: This matters because Game of Darts includes several formats, and not every player remembers every rule. Help content keeps the app usable without forcing players to leave the match-night context.
Players use help when learning a format, checking a setting, or confirming how a feature should work.
bullets: Supports rule discovery and app guidance.
Useful for players switching between formats.
Learn how the visual walkthrough to the full guide and FAQ.

image.alt: Game of Darts help and rules screenshot
image.caption: Help content gives players a reference point for rules and app behaviour.

featureShowcases.heading: Leaderboards and progress
featureShowcases.summary: The leaderboards screen shows how Game Center competition fits alongside local match scoring and personal statistics.
body: It matters because progress is not only about winning one match. Players can compare results, chase personal improvements, and use Game Center where available.
Players use leaderboards after play to compare supported scores and decide what they want to improve next.
bullets: Learn how match results to Game Center leaderboards.
Supports score comparison across supported modes.
Complements the statistics and achievements workflow.

image.caption: Leaderboards give competitive players a reason to keep improving after each match.
